_comment: PK-to-PUA char code mapping for tabbo lute fonts.
Source of truth consumed by both build-woff2-fonts.sh (FontForge encoding step)
and engine/include/pua_map.h (generated from this file, included by Phase 1 json_print).
ASCII zone (pk_code 32-126): glyphs defined in .mf via quoted char literals
(e.g. beginchar("a",...)) stay at their natural Unicode codepoint. Browsers
will not substitute them because the font declares the glyph - only missing
glyphs trigger substitution. These are safe as confirmed in the spike.
PUA zone: all numeric PK codes (0-27, 55-57, 88, 92, 114, 128-255) map to
U+E000 + pk_code. This keeps the relationship trivially traceable (offset 0xE000)
and avoids any Latin-1/Unicode collision for codes 128-255.
Codes not appearing in any .mf beginchar are omitted; unmapped codes that
appear in engine output are a bug and will produce .notdef in the browser.
Regenerate pua_map.h after editing this file:

ascii_zone.description: PK codes 32-126 where MF uses quoted char literal. pk_code == unicode_codepoint.

pua_zone.description: Numeric PK codes mapped to U+E000 + pk_code. pk_code 0 -> U+E000, 128 -> U+E080, 255 -> U+E0FF.
